name: iac-checkov
description: Infrastructure as Code (IaC) security scanning using Checkov with 750+ built-in policies for Terraform, CloudFormation, Kubernetes, Dockerfile, and ARM templates. Use when: (1) Scanning IaC files for security misconfigurations and compliance violations, (2) Validating cloud infrastructure against CIS, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, and SOC2 benchmarks, (3) Detecting secrets and hardcoded credentials in IaC, (4) Implementing policy-as-code in CI/CD pipelines, (5) Generating compliance reports with remediation guidance for cloud security posture management.
capabilities: Scan Terraform files for AWS, Azure, and GCP misconfigurations
Analyze Kubernetes manifests for pod security violations
Detect hardcoded secrets and credentials in IaC files
Generate compliance reports mapped to CIS, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, and SOC2
Create custom security policies for organization requirements
Integrate scanning into GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and pre-commit hooks
author: AgentSecOps
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill with no executable code. Contains YAML configuration templates and markdown guides for Checkov, an open-source IaC security scanner. All 300 static findings are false positives caused by scanner misinterpreting documentation text (check IDs, URLs, security examples) as malicious patterns.
valueStatement: Manual review of Terraform, Kubernetes, and CloudFormation files misses critical misconfigurations. This skill integrates Checkov to automatically scan IaC for 750+ security policies, detecting encryption gaps, overly permissive access, and compliance violations before deployment.
userTitle: Scan Infrastructure as Code for Security
limitations: Does not fix issues automatically - requires manual remediation
Scans static IaC files only, not live cloud infrastructure
Does not scan Terraform state files or plan outputs

useCases.title: CI/CD Security Gates
useCases.description: Block deployments with CRITICAL or HIGH severity issues by integrating Checkov scans into GitHub Actions or GitLab CI pipelines.

useCases.title: Compliance Mapping
useCases.description: Generate reports mapping IaC findings to CIS, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, and SOC2 requirements for audit preparation.

useCases.title: Custom Policy Enforcement
useCases.description: Define organization-specific security policies that extend beyond built-in checks to enforce naming conventions and tagging requirements.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Terraform Scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Run Checkov to scan my Terraform files in the infrastructure/ directory. Show all HIGH and CRITICAL findings with file paths, line numbers, and remediation guidance.

promptTemplates.title: Kubernetes Security
promptTemplates.prompt: Scan Kubernetes manifests in the k8s/ directory using Checkov. Focus on pod security standards, privilege escalation risks, and container security context issues.

promptTemplates.title: Compliance Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a Checkov compliance scan against CIS AWS Foundations benchmark. Generate a JSON report and summarize which controls are passing and failing.

promptTemplates.title: Custom Policy
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me write a custom Checkov policy in Python that ensures all S3 buckets have encryption enabled and are blocked from public access. Include the check logic and how to load it.

bestPractices: Run Checkov on every PR that modifies IaC files to catch issues early
Start with --soft-fail mode to build team awareness before enforcing gates
Use baseline files to track remediation progress without blocking on existing debt
Document legitimate suppressions with ticket numbers and business justification
antiPatterns: Using blanket suppressions (skip-check: *) instead of targeting specific resources
Skipping all LOW severity findings without reviewing them for context
Running scans only in production pipelines instead of developer workflows
Ignoring false positives instead of tuning policies or adding suppressions

faq.question: Does Checkov scan Terraform state files?
faq.answer: No, Checkov scans .tf files and configuration. Use tfsec or Trivy for state file and plan analysis.

faq.question: Which cloud providers are supported?
faq.answer: AWS, Azure, GCP, OCI, Alibaba Cloud, and Kubernetes. Terraform, CloudFormation, ARM, and Docker support.

faq.question: How to integrate with existing CI/CD?
faq.answer: Use the bridgecrewio/checkov-action for GitHub Actions or the Docker image for GitLab CI, Jenkins, and Azure DevOps.

faq.question: Are credentials required to scan IaC?
faq.answer: No, Checkov performs static analysis. Cloud provider credentials are only needed for module downloads.

faq.question: Why am I seeing false positives?
faq.answer: Add inline suppressions with justifications. Update to latest Checkov version which improves accuracy.

faq.question: How does this compare to tfsec or Terrascan?
faq.answer: Checkov has more policies and better multi-cloud support. It uses graph-based analysis to understand resource relationships.
